title: rubus procumbens subsp. subuniflorus
intro: Rubus procumbens subsp. subuniflorus, also known as low-brushing raspberry or woodland raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus procumbens plant, a type of bramble with small, white flowers and edible fruits. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Rubus procumbens subsp. subuniflorus include its ability to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and act as an antioxidant, helping to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from digestive problems to skin conditions.
